bmp2tiff.c_main.summary: This function is a file format conversion utility that converts BMP images to TIFF format, featuring moderate code complexity with approximately 250 lines containing multiple control structures including switch statements, conditionals, and error handling. The algorithm employs sequential file processing with header parsing, color table management for palettized images, and supports both uncompressed and RLE-compressed BMP data through pixel rearrangement and scanline-based writing. Performance is primarily I/O-intensive due to extensive file reading and writing operations, with computational overhead in color space conversion and compression handling. Key technical characteristics include support for multiple BMP variants (Windows and OS/2 formats), flexible compression options (JPEG, LZW, Deflate), dynamic memory allocation for image buffers and color tables, and comprehensive error checking throughout the conversion pipeline.

bmp2tiff.c_rearrangePixels.summary: This function serves as a utility for image format conversion by rearranging pixel data in bitmap buffers, demonstrating low code complexity through its compact structure with a single switch statement and simple loops. It employs basic in-place swapping algorithms to reverse color channel ordering, specifically handling 24-bit and 32-bit pixel formats through efficient pointer arithmetic and temporary variable swaps. The implementation is compute-intensive but memory-efficient, operating directly on buffer data without additional allocations. Key features include its specialized handling of multiple bit depths, conditional processing paths for different pixel formats, and the use of direct memory manipulation for optimal performance in image transformation tasks.

bmp2tiff.c_processCompressOptions.summary: This function serves as a compression option parser within an image format conversion utility, featuring straightforward linear complexity through sequential string comparisons and conditional branching to map input strings to corresponding compression constants. It employs basic string processing algorithms with character scanning for parameter extraction, demonstrating computational lightness with no intensive operations or I/O dependencies. Key characteristics include its support for multiple compression schemes (including JPEG with quality parameters, LZW, and ZIP), robust error handling via a fallback usage call, and modular design for flexible compression configuration in image transformation workflows.

bmp2tiff.c_usage.summary: This utility function serves as a command-line help display mechanism, falling into the category of user interface and program information utilities with low code complexity due to its minimal control structures and straightforward linear execution. gif2tiff.c_makegamtab.summary: This utility function performs gamma correction table generation for image processing applications, featuring straightforward code complexity with only a basic loop structure that implements a mathematical transformation algorithm using power functions to compute gamma-adjusted intensity values. The function is computationally intensive due to the repeated pow() calculations within its 256-iteration loop, with its core technical characteristic being the creation of a pre-computed lookup table (gamtab) that efficiently maps input intensity values through gamma correction for subsequent image processing operations while maintaining precision through rounding via the +0.5 offset.

gif2tiff.c_usage.summary: This utility function serves as a command-line help display mechanism, characterized by low code complexity with minimal control structures and linear execution flow. gif2tiff.c_main.summary: This command-line utility function serves as a GIF to TIFF format converter, demonstrating moderate complexity through its combination of command-line argument parsing, file I/O operations, and user interaction handling. Key technical features include configurable compression options via processCompressOptions(), adjustable rows-per-strip parameters, gamma correction table generation through makegamtab(), and built-in file overwrite protection with interactive user confirmation, all orchestrated through a main conversion routine in convert() that manages the core format transformation.

The function demonstrates I/O-intensive characteristics as it primarily reads from an input file stream, employing a state-machine-like algorithm that processes GIF components in order while maintaining mode state between image blocks, with key features including signature validation, multiple image support through append mode, extension handling, and robust error checking that terminates processing on invalid block types or read failures.

The algorithm employs basic byte-level pattern matching through buffer operations and string comparisons without complex data transformations, resulting in an I/O-bound performance profile where execution time is dominated by the initial file read operation.
